topics_covered: Organizational Details for the Practical Course (PGDP, IN0002)

questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes the relationship between the lecture 'Einführung in die Informatik' (IN0001) and the practical course (IN0002)?

options.label: A
options.text: They are a combined module with one final exam.

options.label: B
options.text: They are two independent modules with separate exams.

options.label: C
options.text: IN0002 is a mandatory part of IN0001.

options.label: D
options.text: One must pass IN0001 before enrolling in IN0002.

questions.explanation: Prof. Westermann explicitly states that the practical course (IN0002) is an independent module with its own examination, and that students may take either or both. They are not a combined unit, not subset, and no prerequisite is mentioned.
questions.source_reference: Lecture and Practical Course as Separate Modules

questions.question_text: In his welcome, Prof. Westermann notes his chair is 'Computergrafik und Visualisierung' and points out its importance for Games Engineering students. What is the most likely reason for this emphasis?

options.label: A
options.text: He is the director of the Games Engineering degree program.

options.label: B
options.text: Computer graphics and visualization are foundational technologies in game development.

options.label: C
options.text: All students in the lecture are required to join a game project.

options.label: D
options.text: His chair only accepts Games Engineering students for thesis work.

questions.explanation: Computer graphics and visualization encompass rendering, animation, and interactive techniques—core components of modern video games. Prof. Westermann highlights this to establish relevance, not because he administers the program or restricts his lab's enrollment.

questions.question_text: Prof. Rüdiger Westermann is the head of the Chair for Computer Graphics and Visualization.
questions.explanation: In his self-introduction, Prof. Westermann states: 'Ich bin Leiter des Lehrstuhls für Computergrafik und Visualisierung.'

questions.question_text: Explain how the lecture 'Einführung in die Informatik' (IN0001) and the practical course (IN0002) are organized in relation to each other.
questions.correct_answer: They are two independent modules with separate examinations. Students may choose to take both, only the lecture, or only the practical course without any binding between them.
questions.explanation: A good answer should mention the independence of the modules, separate assessment, and that no mandatory combination is required. Prof. Westermann underscores this to clarify the flexible enrollment options.

questions.question_text: Based on the introduction, justify why a Games Engineering student would find the chair 'Computergrafik und Visualisierung' and this introductory lecture directly relevant to their studies.
questions.correct_answer: Games Engineering heavily relies on computer graphics (rendering engines, shaders) and visualization (3D modeling, animation, real-time interaction). The professor's expertise in these areas implies that the lecture will address foundational concepts that underpin game technology, making it particularly beneficial for students aiming to work in game development.
questions.explanation: A strong answer connects the chair's specialization to concrete game development topics, demonstrating an understanding that the welcome is not merely a formality but an indication of the course's practical relevance for certain student cohorts.

questions.question_text: According to Prof. Westermann, in what language should students post questions on the Moodle forum?

options.label: A
options.text: German, because the lectures are in German

options.label: B
options.text: English, to accommodate the international teaching staff

options.label: C
options.text: Either German or English is acceptable

options.label: D
options.text: Only in English if the staff member agrees

questions.explanation: Despite the lectures being delivered in German, Prof. Westermann explicitly requests that Moodle forum questions be written in English. This is to ensure that the teaching staff, which may include non-German speakers, can fully understand and address student inquiries.

questions.question_text: The lecture streams are stored and can be accessed by students at any time after they are broadcasted.
questions.explanation: Prof. Westermann states that all lectures and central exercises are streamed via RBG Live and that the streams are saved, allowing students to view them at their convenience.

questions.question_text: Why does Prof. Westermann strongly recommend continuous participation from the very beginning of the course, even for students with prior programming experience?
questions.correct_answer: Because it becomes extremely difficult to catch up in the final weeks if you miss the early material, as later concepts build heavily on the foundations covered in the first two and a half months.
questions.explanation: A good answer should mention the cumulative nature of the course and the risk of falling behind, referencing the professor's warning that catching up in the last three weeks is nearly impossible without consistent engagement from the start.

questions.question_text: Which rooms serve as overflow locations where the lecture is streamed live?

options.label: A
options.text: Only the Friedrich-von-Tiersch-Hörsaal

options.label: B
options.text: Only Hörsaal 1 Interim 1

options.label: C
options.text: Both Friedrich-von-Tiersch-Hörsaal and Hörsaal 1 Interim 1

options.label: D
options.text: There are no overflow rooms; all students attend the main lecture hall

questions.explanation: The lecture specifies two overflow rooms: Friedrich-von-Tiersch-Hörsaal and Hörsaal 1 Interim 1. Both receive a live stream of the lecture simultaneously.

questions.question_text: Students are permitted to respond to exam questions in English, but the questions themselves might be presented only in German.
questions.explanation: Prof. Westermann confirmed that students may answer in English regardless of the question language. However, he expressed uncertainty about whether exam questions would be available in English, implying they may be in German. Hence, the statement accurately reflects the policy as described.

questions.topic: Organizational Details for the Practical Course (PGDP, IN0002)
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about the central exercises (Zentralübungen) is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: All ZÜ2 sessions are streamed and recorded.

options.label: B
options.text: Only the sessions held by Jan Wagner are streamed and recorded.

options.label: C
options.text: Both ZÜ1 and ZÜ2 are always recorded.

options.label: D
options.text: No central exercises are recorded.

questions.explanation: Jan Wagner explicitly mentions that he will stream and record his own sessions, but other parallel sessions held at the same time are not recorded. This was stated when discussing streaming and recordings of Zentralübungen. Options A, C, and D contradict this statement.

questions.topic: Organizational Details for the Practical Course (PGDP, IN0002)
questions.question_text: A student can pass the course even if they fail to achieve the minimum 40% point threshold on one homework sheet, provided they meet the overall passing grade and have at least 9 out of 12 sheets above the threshold.
questions.explanation: The passing criteria require that at least 9 of the 12 graded homework sheets achieve 40% or more of the points, and the overall point total must reach the passing threshold (typically 50–60%). Failing one sheet does not automatically disqualify a student as long as the overall conditions are met. This allows up to three sheets to fall below the per-sheet threshold.

questions.topic: Organizational Details for the Practical Course (PGDP, IN0002)
questions.question_text: Explain the purpose and limitations of the complaint process ('Complaint') for homework assignments.
questions.correct_answer: The complaint process allows students to challenge grading if they believe the correction scheme was applied incorrectly. It is limited to one complaint per assignment and cannot be used to dispute the grading scheme itself or to request a reconsideration of subjective grading decisions. Complaints must be submitted within one week after grades are published.
questions.explanation: A good answer should mention that the complaint is for correcting errors in the application of the grading scheme, not for arguing about the scheme's design. It should also note the time limit and the one-complaint-per-assignment rule. This ensures fairness while preventing abuse.

questions.topic: Organizational Details for the Practical Course (PGDP, IN0002)
questions.question_text: According to the plagiarism policy for homework assignments, which of the following actions is strictly prohibited?

options.label: A
options.text: Citing a small code snippet from Stack Overflow and clearly marking it in the submission.

options.label: B
options.text: Copying a friend's solution and modifying variable names before submitting as your own.

options.label: C
options.text: Using test cases provided by the course instructors to verify your own solution.

options.label: D
options.text: Discussing the general concept of recursion with a study group without sharing code.

questions.explanation: The plagiarism policy strictly prohibits copying another student's code, even with superficial modifications. Option B is a clear violation. Option A is permitted if properly cited (but excessive quoting is not allowed). Option C is acceptable when the instructors encourage sharing tests (as mentioned in the Q&A). Option D is ambiguous but generally discussing concepts without sharing code might be tolerated, but the policy emphasizes individual work; however, the most clearly prohibited action is copying with modification. The policy explicitly states that homework is individual and plagiarism will be penalized.

questions.topic: Organizational Details for the Practical Course (PGDP, IN0002)
questions.question_text: Participating in the ZÜ1 matching process is equivalent to registering for the exam in PGDP.
questions.explanation: The instructor states that the ZÜ1 matching is the binding exam registration. Students who only want to audit the course should not participate in the matching. This is a key organizational detail to prevent accidental exam registration.

questions.question_text: Java was chosen as the teaching language primarily because it is easy to learn, rather than for any industry relevance.
questions.explanation: Prof. Westermann stated that Java is still widely used in industry and that skills in Java are transferable to other languages. The choice is motivated by practical, real-world applicability, not just simplicity.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT among the core topics explicitly listed in the lecture overview?

options.label: A
options.text: Algorithms

options.label: B
options.text: Efficiency analysis

options.label: C
options.text: Data structures

options.label: D
options.text: Compiler design

questions.explanation: The lecture mentions algorithms, efficiency considerations, basic data structures, memory hierarchy, and algorithmic thinking. Compiler design is not part of the listed core topics.

questions.question_text: Briefly explain the 'Informatiker-Denkweise' (computer scientist mindset) as described in the lecture.
questions.correct_answer: It is an approach to problem-solving that seeks not just any solution, but an efficient and computable one, always considering the constraints of the underlying hardware and programming language.
questions.explanation: A good answer should mention that an computer scientist aims for efficiency (not just correctness), and keeps the computer's capabilities and limitations in mind while designing solutions.

questions.question_text: The live 3D game demonstration shown at 60 fps primarily serves to illustrate which overarching concept?

options.label: A
options.text: The capabilities of modern GPU programming

options.label: B
options.text: The necessity of efficient data structures and algorithms in complex software

options.label: C
options.text: How to structure a large Java project

options.label: D
options.text: The basics of physics simulation in games

questions.explanation: Although the demo involves GPU rendering and physics, the lecturer explicitly uses it as a motivation for studying data structures and efficient algorithms. The complexity of a 60 fps game requires careful management of memory, data structures, and computations, which is the core theme.

questions.question_text: According to the lecture, give two reasons why Java is an appropriate language for this course.
questions.correct_answer: Java is widely used in industry and its concepts transfer easily to other programming languages.
questions.explanation: Prof. Westermann argued that Java remains very common in the industry, and learning Java provides a foundation that makes transitioning to other languages relatively easy.
